mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.06% as of Nov 2008 indicates tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recessionary pressure, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and sell equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of Nov 2008 but rising trend evident
regime_hint: post-shock recession with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales measured by RSAFS decline month-over-month by more than 2%, signaling consumer demand collapse and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 686573.0 million as of Nov 2008
regime_hint: demand-side recession with consumer spending collapse
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following RSAFS declines > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declines below 102.0 combined with payroll employment PAYEMS falling below 155,000 thousand persons indicates synchronized supply-side and labor contraction, signaling severe recession and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.01 and PAYEMS at 156105 as of Nov 2008
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction in financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both INDPRO < 102.0 and PAYEMS < 155000, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST fall below 1200 thousand units combined with retail sales RSAFS declining, indicating dual demand collapse in construction and consumption sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1401.0 thousand units as of Oct 2008
regime_hint: housing crisis with spillover to consumer demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both HOUST < 1200 and RSAFS declines, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation measured by CPIAUCSL falls below 300 index points combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% indicates monetary policy lag where real rates remain restrictive despite falling nominal rates, constraining equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 303.29 and FEDFUNDS at 5.06 as of Nov 2008
regime_hint: monetary policy lag in deflationary shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL < 300 and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment PAYEMS declines below 155,000 thousand persons signals labor market contraction that reduces household income and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors anticipate earnings pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156105 thousand as of Nov 2008
regime_hint: labor market contraction in financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when PAYEMS < 155000, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 declines to below 20,200 billion chained 2017 dollars combined with housing starts HOUST falling below 1300 thousand units indicates dual contraction in aggregate demand and housing investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20246.44 billion as of Q3 2008, HOUST at 1401.0 thousand as of Oct 2008
regime_hint: demand-side recession with housing and GDP contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both GDPC1 < 20200 and HOUST < 1300, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
